extensionDescription.message: Select parts of a webpage and schedule browser-based crawling checks. Get alerted only when the value changes.

notice.message: For login-required sites, keep your session signed in. Sites protected by Cloudflare or anti-bot checks may require manual verification to work. Very short repeated checks can cause the site to rate-limit or block your IP/account; you are responsible for that risk.

languageSetupDesc.message: Set this once now. You can change it later with 🌐 in the top-right.

hint.message: Click, then choose from the list. ESC cancels

filterValuesPlaceholder.message: Type a value and press Enter

helpNotificationsMissingMac.message: macOS: System Settings > Notifications > Google Chrome Helper

helpUnexpectedIntro.message: Old allowed sites can continue sending notifications.

helpUnexpectedAction.message: Block the site or remove it from the allowed list.

candidateMenuTitle.message: Choose the exact value below. Different selectors can have the same value.

agentIntro.message: Copy the prompt for an Agent AI. It can inspect the site, choose a selector, and open the command page with a monitor payload.

agentWarning.message: Use Codex, Claude Code, OpenClaw, Antigravity, or another Agent AI that can control your local browser. Normal web GPT, Claude, or Gemini chats cannot register alarms in this extension.

agentPageIntro.message: This page previews a monitor request made by an Agent AI before registering it in the extension.

agentPayloadMissing.message: No agent payload was provided. Open this page with #payload=BASE64URL_JSON.

agentPayloadInvalid.message: The agent payload is invalid. Ask the agent to rebuild the command URL.

agentPreviewMissing.message: Element not found now. It can still alert when it appears later.

agentPromptTemplate.message:
You are an Agent AI that can control a real local browser and Chrome extension pages, such as Codex, Claude Code, OpenClaw, or Antigravity. Do not use a normal web-only GPT/Claude/Gemini chat for this task because it cannot control the user's browser or register the alarm.

Goal: register a Web Change Alert monitor.

Steps:
1. Open the target website in the user's browser. Keep the user's signed-in session if the site requires login.
2. Inspect the page and find the smallest reliable CSS selector for the requested value. Prefer stable ids, classes, and data attributes. Do not include extension picker UI, dropdowns, hints, or temporary overlays in the selected value.
3. Choose extract: {"type":"text"} for visible text, {"type":"image"} for an image URL, {"type":"value"} for form values, or {"type":"attr","attr":"href"} for an attribute.
4. Choose schedule: {"type":"interval","minutes":10}, {"type":"daily","hour":9,"minute":0}, {"type":"weekly","weekday":1,"hour":9,"minute":0}, or {"type":"monthly","day":1,"hour":9,"minute":0}.
5. Choose filter only for text/value monitors: {"mode":"none","values":[]}, {"mode":"only_contains","values":["..."]}, {"mode":"only_missing","values":["..."]}, or {"mode":"only_regex","values":["..."]}.
6. Build JSON like this: {"action":"registerMonitor","url":"https://example.com","selector":".price","extract":{"type":"text"},"schedule":{"type":"interval","minutes":10},"filter":{"mode":"none","values":[]}}.
7. UTF-8 JSON.stringify the JSON, base64url encode it, then open this URL: {commandPageUrl}#payload=ENCODED_JSON
8. The current extension id is {extensionId}. Do not hardcode another extension id. Use the command page URL above.
9. On the command page, check the preview and click Register.
